requirements.summary: Create Sonic-themed search interface with offline capability for franchise content
acceptanceCriteria: A search bar is prominently displayed on the home screen with Sonic-themed branding
Search returns results related to the Sonic franchise (characters, games, media, lore)
Search works offline using previously cached or bundled content
Search results display thumbnails, titles, and short descriptions
Sonic-themed visual styling (colors, icons, fonts) is applied throughout the search UI

file_operations.description: Create the main search page component with a prominent Sonic-themed search bar, search result display area showing thumbnails/titles/descriptions, and offline-first architecture using cached results. Call backend searchSonicContent() and getAllEntriesByType() methods. Display the hero banner image at frontend/public/assets/generated/hero-banner.dim_1200x400.png as a static asset.
Create a reusable Sonic-themed search input component with blue/yellow color scheme, rounded styling, and search icon. Handle user input and trigger search queries.
Create a component to display search results in a grid or list format with thumbnails, titles, and descriptions. Handle empty states and loading states gracefully.
Create a custom hook that wraps backend searchSonicContent() and getAllEntriesByType() calls with React Query for caching and offline support. Implement local storage fallback for offline mode.
Wire SearchPage into the main router with route path '/search' or '/' (home). Ensure navigation includes a link to the search page.

requirements.summary: Build games library with Block Blast puzzle and Sonic runner games, playable offline
acceptanceCriteria: A Games section is accessible from the main navigation
A Block Blast-style tile puzzle game is fully playable in the browser
A Sonic-themed endless runner game is fully playable in the browser
Both games function without an internet connection (offline-first)
Games display a Sonic-themed UI with score tracking
Game library page lists all available games with preview thumbnails

file_operations.description: Create the games library landing page listing available games with preview thumbnails. Display game-thumb-blockblast.dim_256x256.png at frontend/public/assets/generated/game-thumb-blockblast.dim_256x256.png and game-thumb-sonicrunner.dim_256x256.png at frontend/public/assets/generated/game-thumb-sonicrunner.dim_256x256.png as static assets. Link to individual game pages. This is a 2D game section.

Create a fully playable Block Blast-style 2D tile puzzle game component using HTML5 Canvas or CSS Grid. Implement game logic (tile placement, row clearing, scoring) entirely in frontend JavaScript. Use Sonic blue/yellow theming. Track and display score. No backend calls needed for gameplay.

Create a fully playable Sonic-themed endless 2D runner game component using HTML5 Canvas. Implement running/jumping mechanics, obstacle generation, speed increase over time, and score tracking entirely in frontend JavaScript. Use Sonic-themed sprites and colors. No backend calls needed for gameplay.
Create a reusable Canvas wrapper component with Sonic-themed borders, score display overlay, and game controls UI. Accepts game logic as props or children.
Wire GamesPage into router at '/games', BlockBlastGame at '/games/blockblast', and SonicRunnerGame at '/games/sonicrunner'. Add navigation link to Games section in main navigation menu.

requirements.summary: Implement social community feature with age-matched connections, community feed, and moderation with account locking
acceptanceCriteria: Users can be matched with and message other users of the same declared age
A community feed allows users to post short messages visible to others
A moderation panel (admin view) can issue warnings to users
After 3 warnings, a user's account is locked and they are shown a message to create a new account
Locked accounts cannot log in or post
Inappropriate content warnings are displayed on the user's profile

file_operations.description: Create the community feed page that displays posts from createCommunityPost() and getCommunityPosts() backend calls. Show post form for authenticated users. Display community-icon.dim_128x128.png at frontend/public/assets/generated/community-icon.dim_128x128.png as a static asset. Check user accountLocked status from profile before allowing posts.
Create a form component for posting messages to the community feed. Call backend createCommunityPost() method. Disable for locked accounts with a message.
Create a feed component displaying posts with author names, timestamps, and message content. Fetch from backend getCommunityPosts(). Use React Query for caching.

Create a page that calls backend getUsersByAge() to find and display users with matching birth years based on the logged-in user's profile. Show user names and allow initiating conversations (UI placeholder for future messaging).

Create an admin-only moderation panel page that lists users and their warning counts from getUserProfile(). Provide a form to issue warnings via backend issueWarning() method. Use the authorization component to restrict access to admins only; call isCallerAdmin() or getCallerUserRole() to check admin status. Show locked account status.
Create a component displaying a friendly message when a user's account is locked (3 warnings reached). Explain they must create a new account and provide a logout button.
Create a custom hook wrapping getCommunityPosts() backend call with React Query for caching and real-time updates.

Wire CommunityPage into router at '/community', AgeMatcher at '/community/match', and ModerationDashboard at '/admin/moderation'. Add Community link to main navigation. Guard moderation route with admin-only access check.

requirements.summary: Implement age verification on login requiring users to be 10-18 years old
acceptanceCriteria: Every login flow requires the user to confirm their age via a dropdown or input
Ages 10–18 are permitted; any other age results in an access-denied screen
Age verification screen is Sonic-themed and clearly explains the age restriction
Age is stored with the user's session and used for age-matching in social features
No camera-based biometric age detection is used (privacy and reliability concerns)

file_operations.description: Create a Sonic-themed modal that prompts users to enter their name and birth year on first login. Call backend verifyAge() method. Handle AgeCheckResult responses: show access-denied message for tooYoung/tooOld, handle locked accounts, and proceed on ok. Integrate with the authorization component's profile setup flow; check if getCallerUserProfile() returns null to trigger this modal.
Create a Sonic-themed screen displayed when age verification fails (user is outside 10-18 range) or account is locked. Explain the age restriction clearly and provide a logout option.
Create a custom hook wrapping verifyAge() backend call. Parse AgeCheckResult and return user-friendly status. Store verification result in React state.

Integrate AgeVerificationModal to appear after login when getCallerUserProfile() returns null (no profile exists). Show AccessDeniedScreen when age verification fails. Use the authorization component to manage authentication state and trigger profile setup flow.

requirements.summary: Implement parental controls with usage timer, PIN-protected dashboard, and kid mode restrictions
acceptanceCriteria: A parent dashboard exists, accessible via a PIN code
Parents can set a daily usage time limit (in minutes) for their child's account
When the usage timer expires, the child is shown a friendly lockout screen and cannot continue using the app
Users aged 10–12 are automatically placed in 'kid mode' with restricted content
Kid mode hides adult-community features and shows only age-appropriate games and content
Parent dashboard shows the child's total session time for the current day

file_operations.description: Create a PIN-protected parent dashboard page. Display the child's usageTimeRemaining from getCallerUserProfile(). Provide a form to set remaining usage time via backend setRemainingUsageTime() method. Display parental-shield-icon.dim_128x128.png at frontend/public/assets/generated/parental-shield-icon.dim_128x128.png as a static asset. Show session time stats.

Create a modal component for entering a parent PIN code to access the parental dashboard. Store PIN in local storage (basic client-side protection; not cryptographically secure but sufficient for this use case).
Create a friendly Sonic-themed lockout screen displayed when usageTimeRemaining reaches 0. Explain the timer and suggest taking a break. Provide a logout button.

Create a wrapper component that detects if the user is in kid mode (birthYear indicates age 10-12). Hide community features and show only age-appropriate games. Use profile birthYear to calculate current age.

Create a custom hook that tracks usageTimeRemaining from profile, decrements it locally during the session, and calls setRemainingUsageTime() to sync with backend. Trigger lockout when timer reaches 0.

Wire ParentalDashboard into router at '/parental'. Integrate UsageTimerLockout to appear globally when timer expires. Integrate KidModeWrapper to conditionally render navigation/content based on user age. Add parental controls link to settings or navigation.

requirements.summary: Add emergency call button that opens device dialer with emergency number
acceptanceCriteria: An SOS/Emergency button is always visible in the app footer or navigation bar
Tapping it opens the device dialer with an emergency number (e.g., 911) pre-filled using a tel: link
A confirmation prompt asks 'Are you sure you want to call emergency services?' before dialing
The button is styled distinctly (e.g., red) so it is easy to find in an emergency

file_operations.description: Create a prominent red SOS button component displayed in the app footer or navigation. Display sos-icon.dim_128x128.png at frontend/public/assets/generated/sos-icon.dim_128x128.png as a static asset. On click, show a confirmation dialog. If confirmed, open tel:911 link to trigger device dialer. Style with bold red background and white text.
Create a confirmation dialog component asking 'Are you sure you want to call emergency services?' with Yes/No buttons. Trigger tel: link on Yes.
Integrate EmergencyCallButton into the global footer or navigation so it appears on every page.

requirements.summary: Add built-in ad blocker toggle in settings with visual indicator
acceptanceCriteria: App settings include an 'Ad Blocker' toggle that is ON by default
A visible badge or indicator in the UI shows 'Ads Blocked' when enabled
No third-party ad slots or promotional banners appear in the app's content areas when ad blocker is enabled
Toggling the ad blocker off and on persists the preference across sessions

file_operations.description: Create a settings page with an Ad Blocker toggle switch (default ON). Persist toggle state in local storage. Display 'Ads Blocked' badge when enabled. Sonic-themed styling.
Create a reusable toggle component for ad blocker setting. Read/write state from local storage. Display a visual badge or icon when ads are blocked.
Create a custom hook to manage ad blocker state in local storage and provide getter/setter functions.
Wire SettingsPage into router at '/settings'. Add Settings link to main navigation.

requirements.summary: Apply cohesive Sonic-themed visual design across all pages
acceptanceCriteria: Primary colors are Sonic blue (#006DB7), golden yellow (#F5C800), and white with red accents
Typography uses bold, rounded fonts evoking the Sonic game aesthetic
Ring and speed-line decorative elements appear as background or UI accents
All major pages (Home/Search, Games, Community, Settings, Parental Controls) share the same theme
App logo/wordmark 'Scrambly' is displayed on the home screen in Sonic-themed styling

file_operations.description: Define global Sonic color palette CSS variables (--sonic-blue: #006DB7, --sonic-yellow: #F5C800, --sonic-red, --sonic-white). Add global styles for bold rounded typography (e.g., using 'Fredoka One' or similar web font). Include ring and speed-line background patterns or decorative classes.

Extend Tailwind theme with Sonic color palette (sonicBlue, sonicYellow, sonicRed). Configure rounded font family (e.g., 'Fredoka One', sans-serif). Add custom utility classes for speed-line and ring decorative elements.

Create a global layout component wrapping all pages with Sonic-themed header (including scrambly-logo.dim_512x256.png at frontend/public/assets/generated/scrambly-logo.dim_512x256.png as a static asset), navigation bar with links (Search, Games, Community, Settings, Parental, SOS), and footer. Apply consistent Sonic styling (blue/yellow colors, rounded elements, speed-line accents).
Wrap all routes with the Layout component to apply consistent Sonic theming across every page.
